D5 Design L3 Apply Reference

Three Ways to Order Colour

Munsell, Ostwald, and NCS each answer one question differently — what makes two colours equally far apart?

Three great colour-order systems disagree on the most basic question. Munsell (1905) spaces colours by equal perceived steps. Ostwald (1916) spaces them by equal mixtures of white, black, and full colour. NCS (1979) spaces them by resemblance to six elementary percepts. The geometry of each follows from its answer.

Select a system to read its logic. For the 3-D solids these systems build, see the colour-space slicer modules; here the point is the competing philosophy behind each.
